title: Ensure that the proxy file permissions are set to 644 or more restrictive

input_row.title: Ensure that the proxy file permissions are set to 644 or more restrictive
input_row.description: Ensure that the proxy file has permissions of 644 or more restrictive.
input_row.rationale: The proxy file controls various parameters that set the behavior of the kube-proxy service in the worker node. You should restrict its file permissions to maintain the integrity of the file. The file should be writable by only the administrators on the system.
input_row.audit: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on the each worker node. For example, stat -c %a /etc/kubernetes/proxy Verify that the permissions are 644 or more restrictive.
input_row.remediation: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on the each worker node. For example, chmod 644 /etc/kubernetes/proxy Impact: None
input_row.default_value: By default, proxy file has permissions of 644 .

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. SSH into each worker node where kube-proxy is running.
2. Run the command: `stat -c %a /etc/kubernetes/proxy` to check the file permissions of the proxy file.
3. Verify that the permissions returned are 644 or more restrictive (e.g., 600, 640).
4. If the permissions are not 644 or more restrictive, you need to change them by running: `chmod 644 /etc/kubernetes/proxy`.
5. Confirm compliance by re-running the `stat -c %a /etc/kubernetes/proxy` command to ensure the permissions have been updated correctly.
